description: Hidden gems and trending spots from H2 2025 / 2026

description.en: Hyper-local night market with Michelin Bib Gourmand stalls and zero tourist infrastructure. Known for clay oven rolls, ba-wan meatballs, and sesame oil chicken — where Taipei locals actually eat dinner.
description.zh: 超在地夜市，有米其林必比登推薦攤位，幾乎沒有觀光客。以燒餅、肉圓和麻油雞聞名。

address.en: Lane 307, Section 2, Zhonghua Road, Zhongzheng District, Taipei City 100
address.zh: 台北市中正區中華路二段307巷

tips: Stall 072 (Unnamed Clay Oven Rolls) has the longest lines — arrive before 6 PM
Try Aliang Dumplings and Fubin Teppanyaki for under NT$200 dinner
Nearest MRT Xiaonanmen is a 15-min walk via Botanical Garden

description.en: Pure foodie night market with minimal tourist presence. Famous for oyster omelets made with traditional sweet potato starch, taro balls with grass jelly, and traditional meatball soup.
description.zh: 純粹的美食夜市，觀光客少。以蚵仔煎、芋圓仙草和肉圓湯聞名。

address.en: Ningxia Road, Datong District, Taipei City 103

tips: Every second stall smells amazing — just follow your nose
Oyster omelets NT$60-80, taro balls NT$40-60
7-minute walk from Chifeng Street cafes for a great evening combo

description.en: Retro-styled specialty cafe on Chifeng Street with phenomenal pour-over coffee and legendary cinnamon rolls. Located beside Zhongshan Linear Park with a cool vintage interior.
description.zh: 赤峰街上的復古風格精品咖啡店，肉桂捲和手沖咖啡都是一絕。

address.en: No. 25, Lane 49, Chifeng Street, Datong District, Taipei City 104
address.zh: 台北市大同區赤峰街49巷25號

spots.best_time: Weekday afternoons for a quiet seat
tips: Pair coffee with their signature cinnamon roll
South of MRT Shuanglian Station beside the Linear Park
Open 11 AM - 7 PM daily

description.en: Since 2015, a magnet for Taipei's brunch lovers on Chifeng Street. Famous for cinnamon rolls among the city's best, plus ricotta plates, shakshuka, and avocado bagels.
description.zh: 自2015年起就是台北早午餐愛好者的聖地，肉桂捲是全市最佳之一。

address.en: 1F, No. 21, Lane 66, Minsheng West Road, Zhongshan District, Taipei City 104
address.zh: 台北市中山區民生西路66巷21號1樓

spots.best_time: Weekday mornings before 10 AM to avoid queues
tips: NT$300-600 per person for brunch
Their cinnamon rolls sell out — go early
Reservations recommended on weekends

description.en: Hidden in a Japanese-era wooden house on atmospheric Qingtian Street, once home to university professors. One of Taipei's most serene tea experiences surrounded by heritage architecture.
description.zh: 隱藏在青田街日式木造房屋中，曾是大學教授宅邸，氛圍寧靜的茶館體驗。

address.en: Qingtian Street, Da'an District, Taipei City 106

tips: The branching lanes of Qingtian Street are worth exploring on foot
Japanese-era wooden houses create a unique neighborhood atmosphere
Pair with a visit to nearby Yongkang Street for food

description.en: A tea collector's private sanctuary in Zhongshan District run by Vicky Chien. Rare white teas and private oolong collections served in traditional style. Evening reservations include tea-infused cocktails.
description.zh: 中山區的茶藏家私人茶室，由簡小姐經營，提供稀有白茶和烏龍茶收藏。

spots.best_time: By reservation only, evenings for tea cocktails
tips: Reservations are required — contact in advance
Evening sessions include tea-infused cocktails
The owner curates rare oolongs and white teas

description.en: Charming tea house hidden inside a heritage building on Taipei's oldest street, Dihua Street. Dark wooden furniture from different periods of Taiwanese history with Japanese and Taiwanese serveware.
description.zh: 隱藏在迪化街歷史建築中的茶館，擁有不同時期的台灣木製家具。

address.en: Dihua Street, Datong District, Taipei City 103

tips: Located on Taipei's oldest commercial street — explore the surroundings
Serveware sourced from Japan and Taiwan adds to the experience
Combine with a walk through Dihua Street's fabric and dried goods shops

description.en: Scandinavian-inspired specialty coffee shop in Zhongshan — one of the few offering Taiwanese single-origin beans for filter coffee. Clean Nordic aesthetics with serious coffee credentials.
description.zh: 中山區北歐風格精品咖啡店，少數提供台灣單品咖啡豆手沖的店家之一。

spots.best_time: Morning for the best filter coffee selection
tips: Try their Taiwanese single-origin pour-over
The Nordic design aesthetic makes it a calm workspace
Near Zhongshan MRT station

description.en: Insanely popular hidden cafe near Dihua Street that's open until midnight. A crew of regulars from students to night owls gather here — true local Taipei nightlife coffee culture.
description.zh: 迪化街附近的隱藏咖啡店，營業到午夜，從學生到夜貓子都聚集在此。

address.en: Near Dihua Street, Datong District, Taipei City 103
address.zh: 台北市大同區迪化街附近

spots.best_time: 9:00 PM - midnight for the real vibe
tips: Open until midnight daily — rare for Taipei cafes
Popular with local students and creatives
Near historic Dihua Street

description.en: Has overtaken Shilin as Taipei's top night market. Signature Fuzhou pepper buns baked in clay ovens are unavailable at Shilin. Adjacent to impressive six-story Songshan Ciyou Temple.
description.zh: 已超越士林成為台北頂級夜市，招牌福州胡椒餅在士林吃不到。旁邊有松山慈祐宮。

address.en: Raohe Street, Songshan District, Taipei City 105

tips: Fuzhou pepper buns (胡椒餅) NT$50-70 — queue forms early
Go up all six floors of Songshan Ciyou Temple at the entrance
Less chaotic layout than Shilin — single long street

description.en: Student-favorite night market near National Taiwan University campus. Students always know the cheapest and tastiest food — prices are the lowest in Taipei.
description.zh: 台大學生最愛的夜市，學生總是知道最便宜最好吃的食物。

address.en: Gongguan area, Zhongzheng District, Taipei City 100
address.zh: 台北市中正區公館地區

tips: Near NTNU campus — follow the students for the best stalls
Prices cheaper than Raohe or Shilin
MRT Gongguan Station exit 4

description.en: Housed in the opulent former US Embassy building from the 1920s, combining art-house cinema with a garden cafe. Perfect for coffee or catching local and international independent films.
description.zh: 位於1920年代前美國大使館建築內，結合獨立電影院和花園咖啡廳。

address.en: No. 18, Section 2, Zhongshan North Road, Zhongshan District, Taipei City 104
address.zh: 台北市中山區中山北路二段18號

spots.best_time: Afternoon for cafe, evening for films
tips: The garden cafe is a peaceful escape from the busy street
Check their film schedule for indie screenings
Beautiful 1920s colonial architecture worth seeing alone

description.en: Transformed from an industrial auto-parts alley into Taipei's most creative neighborhood. Old iron workshops became minimalist cafes, hardware stores turned into indie boutiques and art studios.
description.zh: 從汽車零件巷弄轉型為台北最具創意的街區，鐵工廠變成咖啡店，五金行變成獨立精品店。

address.en: Chifeng Street, between MRT Zhongshan and Shuanglian stations, Datong District, Taipei City 104
address.zh: 台北市大同區赤峰街（中山站至雙連站之間）

spots.best_time: Weekday afternoons; Saturday evenings for pop-up craft stalls
tips: Exit MRT Zhongshan Station Exit 5, turn right, first right is Chifeng Street
Saturday evenings have pop-up craft stalls and buskers
End at Ningxia Night Market (7 min walk) for dinner

description.en: Tea-growing hillside region on Taipei's edge with 40+ traditional teashops. Home to Baozhong and Tieguanyin oolongs — one lighter, one darker and roasted. Reached by gondola.
description.zh: 台北邊緣的產茶山區，有40多家傳統茶館，出產包種和鐵觀音烏龍茶。搭纜車可達。

spots.best_time: Afternoon to sunset for tea with mountain views
tips: Take the Maokong Gondola from Taipei Zoo MRT station
Try both Baozhong (light oolong) and Tieguanyin (roasted oolong)
Over 40 teahouses to choose from — pick one with a valley view

description.en: One of the cutest and most photogenic cafes on Chifeng Street with vintage vibes. Popular with young locals and MZ generation visitors drawn to the aesthetic and specialty drinks.
description.zh: 赤峰街上最可愛的網美咖啡店之一，復古風格吸引年輕人和MZ世代。

address.en: Chifeng Street, Datong District, Taipei City 104

tips: Highly Instagrammable vintage interior
Walk the full length of Chifeng Street to discover more gems
Near Coffee Dumbo — do both in one visit

name.zh: 華山1914文化創意產業園區

description.en: Former winery turned creative arts hub with rotating exhibitions, artisan markets, indie concerts, and innovative food concepts. Taipei's creative pulse in a heritage industrial space.
description.zh: 前酒廠改建的文創園區，有展覽、市集、獨立音樂會和創新美食概念。

address.en: No. 1, Section 1, Bade Road, Zhongzheng District, Taipei City 100
address.zh: 台北市中正區八德路一段1號

spots.best_time: Weekends for markets, weekdays for quiet exhibitions
tips: Check their exhibition schedule before visiting
Weekend artisan markets showcase local makers
Great for a rainy day activity
